name: Wazuh
what: Open-source security platform / SIEM/XDR-style stack for log analysis, endpoint monitoring, integrity checks, and compliance use cases.
solves: Security monitoring, alerting, endpoint telemetry, and central security visibility.
users: Security and infrastructure teams operating many hosts/services.
fit: Useful later if Hylono’s self-hosted footprint grows enough to justify centralized security operations. Not a first-wave requirement.
runtime: Manager/indexer/dashboard/agent architecture with significant operational footprint.
components: Agents, manager, indexer, dashboard, rules, and security analytics pipelines.
deploy: Self-hostable but operationally heavy. Best introduced when there are enough systems to secure centrally.
deps: Compute/storage, agent management, network design, rules tuning, and security operations ownership.
auth: Dashboard/admin auth and agent trust relationships.
apis: Security APIs, agent communication, alerting, and dashboards.
security: Security tooling itself becomes critical infrastructure; access and update hygiene matter.
setup_path: Defer until the platform footprint and security maturity justify centralized SIEM/EDR-style tooling.
env: Cluster/indexer settings, certificates, agent enrollment, dashboard auth, and storage sizing.
persistence: Security logs and indexes require retention policy and storage planning.
operations: Rule tuning, false positives, agent lifecycle, and incident workflows dominate.
failure_points: Deploying SIEM tooling before there is a team/process to operate it.
integration: Could later cover hosts running databases, gateways, app clusters, and critical services.
verify: Decide when Hylono’s infra/security maturity is ready for central security operations.
gdpr: Security logs can contain personal/system identifiers; retention/access matter.
overlap: Some overlap with cloud-native security monitoring, but broader self-host visibility if adopted.
codex_read_first: Read the security-operations maturity notes before recommending Wazuh.
codex_best_path: Defer until there is enough infrastructure and ownership to justify it.
codex_never_assume: Never deploy SIEM/EDR-style platforms without people/process to run them.
